---
title: "Methods"
---

::: {.callout-tip}
## Learning Goals: Methods
1. Be able to "navigate" the brain using the following terms: left hemisphere, right hemisphere, frontal, temporal, parietal, occipital, anterior, posterior, inferior, superior, medial, ventral, sulcus, gyrus
1. Identify and distinguish coronal, axial, and sagittal sections of the human brain
1. Understand the difference between spatial and temporal resolution
1. Know the names of the major research tools that are used to study language in the brain and what is measured by each tool
1. Understand how scientific papers are meant to be read
:::


| Date | Topic | Reading | Slides | Poll |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| Thurs 9/3 | Methods | SGN Ch. 2: *The Toolbox*  |  [<span class="blue-badge">Methods & Interpretation ↗</span>](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1-DlaIOIvnQBYJNo8rJH51RvTGDmv6JFJAnk2MSbpzUQ/edit?slide=id.p#slide=id.p)| [Poll](https://www.menti.com/blzw41uh6xjx)
| Tues 9/8 | *No class (Monday schedule)* | | | 
| Thurs 9/10 | Interpreting brain data | McCabe & Castel 2008 |


<!-- Mentimeter
intro: temp check

1. Label the lobes
label, one at a time:
* lobes
* anterior, posterior, inferior, superior, medial, ventral

how much did you understand of today's class?
muddiest point?
-->
